discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with semis (SMH +2.6%) and mega-cap tech leading; VIX down to 18.76. Mid-cap divergence notable: CALX and EXTR drilled on networking weakness while MANH and WEX spike on earnings. Iran/Hormuz oil disruption keeps energy bid but broader market shrugging.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2.62% and AVGO/TSM both +5% suggest semi/AI capex narrative reaccelerating
ITA -1.79% weak despite risk-on tape — worth tracking given Iran backdrop
XLF modestly red despite risk-on; KRE -0.56% hints at rate/credit concerns
XLE +1.18% with oil above $101 on Hormuz tanker attacks

discoveries.name: Calix, Inc
discoveries.mechanism: Likely earnings/guidance reaction (4x volume, -14% into a +1% tape). Without the release in hand, hard to call overdone vs rational, but a -14% gap on a small-cap networking/broadband play often overshoots if guide was merely soft rather than structurally broken.
research_pointers: Pull Calix Q1 release and listen to call — specifically BEAD/RDOF commentary and FY guide
Compare to prior quarter's commentary on carrier inventory digestion
Check short interest and whether this breaks below prior post-earnings lows
Read across to EXTR weakness — is there a common carrier capex message?
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Guide cut reflects structural share loss or material customer concentration issue, not cyclical digestion — in which case -14% may be rational or even insufficient.

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks
discoveries.mechanism: Possible sympathy with CALX on enterprise/networking capex fear, OR independent guidance issue. 3x volume suggests news-driven, not pure sympathy.
discoveries.catalyst: Verify if EXTR reported or pre-announced; otherwise sympathy to CALX
research_pointers: Confirm whether EXTR has its own news or is trading in sympathy
If pure sympathy, EXTR historically serves different end markets (enterprise campus) than CALX (broadband access) — divergence could be mispricing
Check CSCO, JNPR, ANET tape for read-through
discoveries.what_would_falsify: EXTR has its own negative pre-announcement or guidance cut, making the move company-specific and rational.

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates
discoveries.mechanism: Up 10.9% over 5 days on 2x volume suggests earnings beat/raise. Supply chain software demand inflecting with tariff refund/re-routing dynamics.
research_pointers: Confirm Q1 print and RPO/cloud subscription growth
Management commentary on macro/tariff-driven supply chain software demand
Valuation check — MANH trades at premium multiple; how much is priced in
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Beat was driven by one-time items or guide was only reiterated not raised.

discoveries.name: Repligen
discoveries.mechanism: Only 1.16x volume on -6.25% — move is meaningful but not on heavy distribution. Could be single-seller, analyst downgrade, or pre-earnings de-risking. XBI is +0.67%, so not sector sympathy.
discoveries.catalyst: No obvious news in provided feed
research_pointers: Check for analyst actions, biopharma bioprocessing peer commentary (DHR, TMO, SRT)
Any China/tariff exposure read-through to bioprocessing tools
Upcoming earnings date and prior guide cadence
discoveries.what_would_falsify: A specific downgrade or negative customer data-point confirms company-specific rational selling.

discoveries.name: Best Buy
discoveries.mechanism: -4.6% on only 1.07x volume in a +1% tape is notable given XLY flat. No company-specific news in feed. Tariff refund headlines today could be read as a negative for retailers who'd be expected to rebate customers, though that's a stretch.
discoveries.catalyst: Possibly tariff-refund mechanics creating uncertainty for big-box importers, or reaction to a competitor data point
research_pointers: Check for any AAPL/consumer electronics price guidance
Any credit-card/delinquency data today impacting big-ticket discretionary
Tariff refund process — is BBY a net beneficiary or does it create customer rebate liability?
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Specific analyst downgrade or channel check explains the move fully.

discoveries.name: WEX Inc.
discoveries.mechanism: Up 8% over 5 days, 2x volume — likely earnings-driven. Fleet/fuel card exposure may benefit from higher oil if spreads hold.
discoveries.catalyst: Probable Q1 earnings or raised guidance
research_pointers: Confirm earnings print, fuel price sensitivity commentary
Fleet segment volume trends vs. trucking macro
Short interest / any activist commentary
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Move driven by buyback announcement or one-off, not operating trends.

catalyst_chains.primary_event: CALX -14% on presumed broadband/access capex weakness
Iran seizes tankers in Hormuz, oil back above $101

secondary_opportunities.relationship: fuel-card spread beneficiary (mixed — higher prices help revenue but hurt volume)
defense — surprisingly weak despite geopolitics, worth investigating why

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Trump reportedly ready to reclassify cannabis

secondary_opportunities.relationship: US MSO — larger beneficiary from banking access

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump posts repeatedly attacking SCOTUS tariff refund decision ($159B) and hinting at dissatisfaction with Republican-appointed justices; no new policy announced.
trump_signals.note: Rhetoric confirms tariff refunds are proceeding despite administration resistance. Refund portal friction (per MarketWatch) suggests the refund process will be slow/messy — companies counting on quick cash-back may see working capital lag. Watch for executive actions attempting to blunt refund scope. Low actionable signal tonight; monitor for any 'we'll find another way' policy escalation.

trump_signals.post_summary: Spirit Airlines rescue package reportedly in advanced talks
trump_signals.note: Not a Trump post but administration action. Rescue is positive for SAVE bondholders/equity, marginally negative for budget-carrier competitors (JBLU, ULCC) who lose a distressed-capacity tailwind.

discovery.no_signals_note: Tesla earnings after the bell — results could move TSLA, auto suppliers, and lithium names. Several mid-cap movers (CALX, EXTR, MANH, WEX, RGEN) need earnings release confirmation before conviction improves.

ai_announcements.headline: Google turns Chrome into an AI co-worker; Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform; AI Overviews in Gmail
ai_announcements.summary: Google Cloud Next 2026 announcements: Chrome auto-browse for enterprise, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform for IT users, AI Overviews in Gmail, AI in Google Maps.
ai_announcements.note: No clear mapping from today's movers to these announcements. CALX, EXTR are networking hardware (likely earnings/guidance-driven, not AI news); MANH up on application software (possibly benefits from AI workflow tailwinds but no direct catalyst in feed); RGEN healthcare; WEX fintech; BBY/GME retail.

ai_announcements.headline: Google Cloud launches two new TPUs to compete with Nvidia
ai_announcements.summary: New Google TPUs positioned as faster/cheaper than prior gen; Google still embracing Nvidia for now (Thinking Machines Lab deal uses GB300).

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco supplies semicap equipment (MOCVD, ion beam, laser annealing) used in advanced logic/memory/packaging. More TPU + GPU capacity = more advanced-node and HBM capacity = more tool demand. TPU proliferation is net positive regardless of Nvidia share.
research_pointers: Veeco exposure to HBM/advanced packaging capex
Whether 5-day +18% already reflects AI semicap rally
Check for any separate VECO-specific catalyst (earnings, order)

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI + Infosys partnership
ai_announcements.summary: Integration focused on software dev, legacy modernization, DevOps for Infosys clients.

ai_announcements.headline: Unauthorized group allegedly accessed Anthropic's Mythos cyber tool
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic investigating; says no evidence systems impacted. FLAGGED AS ANTHROPIC-RELATED FOR BIAS GRADING.
ai_announcements.note: No obvious public stock read-through in today's movers. If confirmed, could be marginal positive for cyber names (PANW, CRWD, S) on increased enterprise concern about AI tool security, but none appear in mover list.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most large movers today (CALX -14%, EXTR -5.8%, RGEN -6.3%, BBY -4.6%, MANH +5.9%, WEX +4.3%) do not have a clear linkage to the AI announcements in the feed. CALX's -14% on 4x volume with no AI-announcement tie suggests earnings/guidance — not an AI-sentiment mispricing. Recommend Michael look at earnings calendar rather than treating these as AI-reaction trades. VECO's move is directionally consistent with Google TPU news (more AI capex = more semicap demand) and is RATIONAL, not an opportunity.
